frontmatter_compliance.notes: All required fields present and well-formed; disable-model-invocation: true is not in the frontmatter spec — needs spec alignment or move to metadata.

description_quality.notes: 819 chars; starts with 'Bootstrap'; 5 trigger phrases plus slash-command form; clear scope statement.

progressive_disclosure.notes: Body 125 lines; 10 small templates under references/templates/ all linked from body lines 66-68.

instruction_clarity.notes: Three-question flow with per-question heuristics; idempotence rules explicit; failure-loud contract for downstream skills clearly stated.

coordination.notes: owns.directories docs/agents/ exclusive; composes_with project-profiler + sync-skills both exist; compose-with section accurate.

completeness.notes: All 10 templates exist; Agent skills block given verbatim; downstream consumer pattern documented.

anti_patterns.notes: disable-model-invocation is an out-of-spec extension; MUST in failure-loud contract is justified. Hardcoded 'Skill-Madness toolkit' name acceptable.

important.item: disable-model-invocation: true frontmatter field is not in the spec
important.fix: Document it in skills/meta/skill-writer/references/frontmatter-spec.md or move to metadata.invocation: explicit-only

nits.item: Hardcoded 'Skill-Madness toolkit' name in body text
nits.fix: Acceptable since project is the target; leave or parameterize

nits.item: Output mapping uses shorthand bullets where a table would be clearer
Failure-loud contract uses MUST but lacks a concrete detection snippet for downstream skill authors
nits.fix: Add a one-line example check + error message template

top_fixes.fix: Resolve out-of-spec disable-model-invocation: true frontmatter field (add to spec or move under metadata)
Convert answer→template mapping bullets to explicit table
Add a concrete downstream detection-pattern code snippet
